title: Wildly Smoked Polish Pork Dumplings
description: A traditional Polish dish featuring smoked pork and rice paper sheets, served in individual mini cocottes. This recipe is onion and garlic free, making it suitable for those with dietary restrictions.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the ground pork, sliced mushrooms, sour cream, dried marjoram, salt, and black pepper, and mix well.
Lay a rice paper sheet on a flat surface and place a tablespoon of the pork mixture in the center of the sheet.
Fold the bottom half of the rice paper sheet over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll up the sheet to form a tight cylinder.
Repeat the process with the remaining rice paper sheets and pork mixture.
Place the dumplings in individual mini cocottes and add a tablespoon of red wine to each cocotte.
Cover the cocottes with lids and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove the lids and bake for an additional 10 minutes, or until the dumplings are cooked through and the rice paper is crispy.
